steps.title: Identify the defining claim
steps.content:
Young and colleagues recommend a new space telescope “dedicated exclusively to solar-system observations.” The word exclusively signals that current resources are not primarily devoted to this work.

The passage supports this with concrete data: only 6% of HST observations are of solar-system bodies, meaning the remaining 94% are extrasolar.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly captures what the recommendation implies: that HST's dedicated focus is extrasolar.

The other choices either contradict explicit passage facts (D claims HST lacks UV sensors when it revolutionized UV imaging), overstate the recommendation (B implies complete discontinuation), or misrepresent what Young and colleagues want (C proposes modification when they advocate for a new dedicated instrument).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
